spec_match.rationale: Computes a font size by dividing container width by a baseline length to get a unitless ratio, then re-multiplying — canonical typed arithmetic application.

interactive.rationale: Resizable frame that drives the headline live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a clamp-free, breakpoint-free fluid type rule; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens at very small / very large container widths — does it explode or clamp? The implicit interpolation has no fallback.

openQuestions.title: Compare with clamp()
openQuestions.detail: The clamp() approach is the established pattern. Render the same headline with both approaches and show the clamp() floor/ceiling vs the typed-arithmetic linear interpolation.

openQuestions.title: Show extreme widths
openQuestions.detail: What's the font size at 200px container? At 4000px? Either clamp via min/max or show the unbounded interpolation result so authors understand the trade-off vs clamp().

summary: Beautiful resize-driven demo. To compete with clamp(), explicitly compare the two approaches and show extreme-width behaviour so authors can pick the right tool.
